title: Fix race condition in memcached-pod-kill chaos test
description:
# GitHub Issue #214: Fix race condition in memcached-pod-kill chaos test that causes flaky failures

**Category**: bug | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: The `keystone-memcached-pod-kill` chaos test in `tests/e2e-chaos/memcached-pod-kill/chainsaw-test.yaml` races with Chaos Mesh pod deletion and fails non-deterministically. In step-4 the test uses `wait` with a label selector to confirm the killed pod becomes `Ready=false` before asserting Keystone stays `Ready=True`. chainsaw/kubectl `wait` resolves the label selector once up front, grabs the doomed pod (e.g. `openstack-memcached-75845cbcd5-ht4h9`), and watches that specific object. When Chaos Mesh deletes the pod mid-wait, the watch errors with `Error from server (NotFound): pods "openstack-memcached-75845cbcd5-ht4h9" not found` instead of re-resolving the selector onto the replacement pod the ReplicaSet immediately creates. Observed in run https://github.com/C5C3/forge/actions/runs/24412091069/job/71314792352: the replacement pod `openstack-memcached-75845cbcd5-ptznn` was already `Scheduled`/`Created`/`Started` within the same second (visible in the event dump), the memcached-operator Deployment was healthy, and the Keystone CR ended the test with `Ready=True/AllReady` — confirming this is a test-only race, not an operator regression. The `NOTE` block at lines 50-56 of `chainsaw-test.yaml` already anticipates this race, but the documented mitigation ("pod-kill with gracePeriod:0 plus controller restart takes multiple seconds") does not hold in practice — the Pending→Scheduled→Created transition for the replacement pod happens at the same timestamp as the delete.

**Motivation**: The `keystone-mariadb-pod-kill` and `keystone-openbao-pod-kill` tests in the same suite pass reliably because they assert on Keystone CR sub-conditions (e.g. `DatabaseReady=False`), which chainsaw retries internally until they match. The memcached test cannot use that pattern by design — cache outages are treated as performance degradation only and must not flip any Keystone condition (CC-0047, SC-CHAOS-002). The current workaround (pod-level `Ready=false` wait) introduces a structural race that produces false negatives in CI, blocks the chaos test suite (issue #165), and hides real regressions in the noise.

**Affected Areas**:
- `tests/e2e-chaos/memcached-pod-kill/chainsaw-test.yaml`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `keystone-memcached-pod-kill` no longer relies on `wait` with `condition: Ready=false` against a label selector that matches the pod being deleted
- [ ] The chaos effect is verified via a mechanism that is resilient to pod replacement, e.g. asserting on `Deployment.status.unavailableReplicas > 0` or on a `Pod` UID/`resourceVersion` change, both of which chainsaw retries
- [ ] Alternatively, the `Ready=false` wait is dropped entirely and the test relies only on the subsequent `Ready=true` wait plus the Keystone `AllReady` assertion — which is sufficient to validate the test's actual goal (memcached failures do not degrade Keystone)
- [ ] The `NOTE` block at lines 50-56 is removed or rewritten to reflect the new approach
- [ ] The test passes on three consecutive CI runs of `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` (e2e-chaos job)
- [ ] No change is required in the memcached-operator repository — this is a pure test fix

**Labels:** bug

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/214

stories.title: CI engineer gets reliable memcached-pod-kill chaos test results
stories.want: the keystone-memcached-pod-kill chaos test to pass deterministically without flaky NotFound errors from kubectl wait racing with pod deletion
stories.so_that: CI results accurately reflect operator behavior and flaky failures do not block the chaos test suite or hide real regressions
criteria: Test passes when pod replacement happens within the same second as pod deletion (the observed race scenario from GitHub Actions run 24412091069)
Test does not use chainsaw wait with condition=Ready=false on Pods matching a label selector that targets the pod being deleted
Test uses Deployment-level readyReplicas polling instead of Pod-level condition watching, following the operator-pod-crash and api-pod-kill-pdb patterns
Test passes on three consecutive CI runs of the e2e-chaos job

stories.title: CI engineer can verify chaos injection took effect before operator assertions
stories.want: the test to confirm that Chaos Mesh actually killed a memcached pod before asserting Keystone operator conditions
stories.so_that: a Chaos Mesh misconfiguration (selector not matching any pods) does not produce a false-positive test pass
criteria: The script polls the memcached Deployment readyReplicas and detects the drop below desired replicas (Phase 1)
The script explicitly fails with ERROR and prints current readyReplicas if chaos did not take effect within the poll timeout
The script explicitly fails with ERROR if memcached does not recover within the recovery timeout (Phase 2)

stories.title: Test maintainer finds documentation consistent with test implementation
stories.want: the chaos-e2e-tests reference documentation to accurately describe the memcached-pod-kill step-4 mechanics after the race condition fix
stories.so_that: developers modifying or debugging the test can trust the documentation
criteria: docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md memcached-pod-kill step table reflects script-based Deployment polling instead of Pod-level wait
The Design note is updated to reference CC-0076 and the Deployment-level approach
The NOTE block about theoretical race and initial-sleep mitigation is no longer referenced

requirements.description: Step-4 SHALL NOT use chainsaw wait with condition=Ready=false on Pods matching a label selector that targets the pod being deleted by Chaos Mesh
requirements.rationale: kubectl wait resolves the label selector once at query time, watches the specific pod object, and errors with NotFound when Chaos Mesh deletes the pod mid-watch — this is the root cause of the observed race condition (issue #214)

scenarios.name: Pod deleted during observation window does not cause NotFound
scenarios.when: Chaos Mesh kills the memcached pod and the ReplicaSet creates a replacement within the same second
scenarios.then: the test does not error with 'pods not found' because it watches the Deployment object (which persists), not individual Pod objects
and_then: the test continues to the Keystone condition assertions

scenarios.name: No wait operations on pods via label selector in step 4
scenarios.then: step 4 contains no wait sub-steps with kind=Pod and a label selector
and_then: the wait condition=Ready=true on Pods is also removed and replaced by the script

requirements.description: Step-4 SHALL verify chaos took effect via Deployment-level readyReplicas polling, resilient to pod replacement
requirements.rationale: Deployment.status.readyReplicas aggregates all pod states and the Deployment object persists through pod replacements. The operator-pod-crash (lines 62-74) and api-pod-kill-pdb (lines 79-91) tests use this proven pattern.

scenarios.name: Chaos effect detected via readyReplicas drop
scenarios.when: Chaos Mesh kills a memcached pod and the Deployment readyReplicas drops below desired
scenarios.then: the script detects the drop within the poll timeout and logs a confirmation message
and_then: the script proceeds to Phase 2 (recovery polling)

scenarios.name: Chaos effect not detected within timeout
scenarios.when: the pod kill does not take effect (e.g., Chaos Mesh misconfiguration) and readyReplicas stays at desired
scenarios.then: the script exits with ERROR and prints the current readyReplicas
and_then: the catch block runs diagnostics.sh for troubleshooting

requirements.description: Step-4 SHALL verify memcached Deployment recovery (readyReplicas returns to desired) before asserting Keystone conditions
requirements.rationale: Confirming recovery ensures the Keystone assertions run against a stable cluster state. Both operator-pod-crash and api-pod-kill-pdb follow this two-phase pattern.

scenarios.name: Recovery detected via readyReplicas return
scenarios.when: the replacement memcached pod passes readiness and readyReplicas returns to desired
scenarios.then: the script logs a confirmation and exits 0
and_then: the Keystone condition assert sub-step runs

scenarios.name: Recovery timeout
scenarios.when: readyReplicas does not return to desired within the recovery poll timeout
scenarios.then: the script exits with ERROR and prints the current readyReplicas

requirements.description: Step-4 SHALL assert all 6 Keystone conditions remain True (SecretsReady, FernetKeysReady, DatabaseReady, DeploymentReady, BootstrapReady, Ready=AllReady) after memcached pod kill — unchanged from the original test
requirements.rationale: This is the test's primary assertion — memcached failures are treated as performance degradation only and must not flip any Keystone condition (CC-0047, SC-CHAOS-002)

scenarios.name: All 6 conditions remain True during memcached outage
scenarios.when: a memcached pod is killed and replaced
scenarios.then: the Keystone CR shows all 6 conditions as True with Ready reason AllReady

scenarios.name: Assert block is identical to original
scenarios.when: the assert sub-step in step 4 is compared to the original test
scenarios.then: the 6-condition JMESPath assertions are preserved verbatim

requirements.description: The NOTE block (original lines 49-53) SHALL be removed and the step-4 comment SHALL describe the Deployment-level polling approach with CC-0076 reference
requirements.rationale: The NOTE described a theoretical race with a mitigation (initial sleep) that failed in practice. The Deployment-level approach eliminates the race, making the NOTE obsolete.

scenarios.name: NOTE block removed
scenarios.then: there is no comment block describing a theoretical race or suggesting an initial sleep mitigation

scenarios.name: New comment describes approach
scenarios.when: the step-4 comment block is read
scenarios.then: it explains Deployment-level readyReplicas polling, references CC-0076 and issue #214, and notes the pattern origin (operator-pod-crash / api-pod-kill-pdb)

requirements.description: docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md SHALL reflect the updated step-4 mechanics for the memcached-pod-kill test suite
requirements.rationale: Documentation must match the actual test implementation for maintainability. Step 4 description and Design note currently reference Pod-level wait operations that no longer exist.

scenarios.name: Step table updated
scenarios.when: the memcached-pod-kill steps table (line 198) is read
scenarios.then: step 4 describes script-based Deployment readyReplicas polling instead of wait (2m) + assert (5m)

scenarios.name: Design note updated
scenarios.when: the Design note below the steps table (lines 208-210) is read
scenarios.then: it describes the Deployment-level polling approach and references the race condition fix (CC-0076)

tasks.title: Replace step-4 wait operations with Deployment-level readyReplicas polling script in chainsaw-test.yaml (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-005)
tasks.description: In tests/e2e-chaos/memcached-pod-kill/chainsaw-test.yaml step 4: (1) Replace the step-4 comment block (lines 44-48) and NOTE block (lines 49-53) with a new comment explaining Deployment-level polling, referencing CC-0076/issue #214 and the operator-pod-crash/api-pod-kill-pdb pattern. (2) Replace the two wait sub-steps (lines 54-72: wait Ready=false then wait Ready=true on Pods) with a single script sub-step that follows the operator-pod-crash pattern (lines 54-85 of operator-pod-crash/chainsaw-test.yaml): Phase 1 polls readyReplicas dropping below desired (chaos confirmed), Phase 2 polls readyReplicas returning to desired (recovery confirmed). Use the memcached Deployment name openstack-memcached in $NAMESPACE. Omit set -euo pipefail (same rationale as operator-pod-crash: ${READY:-0} defaults are incompatible with set -e). Use 120s script timeout. (3) Keep the existing assert sub-step (lines 73-93: all 6 Keystone conditions) and catch block (lines 94-96) completely unchanged.

tasks.title: Update docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md memcached-pod-kill section to reflect new step-4 approach (REQ-006)
tasks.description: In docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md: (1) Update the memcached-pod-kill step 4 row in the steps table (line 198) — change Type from 'wait (2m) + assert (5m)' to 'script (120s) + assert (5m)' and update Details to describe Deployment readyReplicas polling for chaos confirmation and recovery, followed by all 6 condition assertions. (2) Update the Design note (lines 208-210) to describe the Deployment-level approach, reference CC-0076, and explain why Pod-level wait was replaced (label-selector race with pod deletion).

test_specifications.test_function: step-4 script Phase 1: readyReplicas drop detection
test_specifications.story: CI engineer can verify chaos injection took effect before operator assertions
test_specifications.expected: [e2e] Script polls memcached Deployment readyReplicas via kubectl jsonpath and detects the drop below desired within 60s (30 iterations * 2s). Exits with ERROR if readyReplicas never drops.

test_specifications.test_function: step-4 script Phase 2: readyReplicas recovery detection
test_specifications.story: CI engineer gets reliable memcached-pod-kill chaos test results
test_specifications.expected: [e2e] Script polls memcached Deployment readyReplicas and detects recovery to desired within 120s (60 iterations * 2s). Exits 0 on recovery, exits 1 with ERROR on timeout.

test_specifications.test_function: step-4 assert: all 6 Keystone conditions True
test_specifications.story: CI engineer gets reliable memcached-pod-kill chaos test results
test_specifications.expected: [e2e] Chainsaw assert retries until all 6 Keystone conditions (SecretsReady, FernetKeysReady, DatabaseReady, DeploymentReady, BootstrapReady, Ready=AllReady) match True, within the 5m assert timeout. Identical to original test assertions.

test_specifications.test_function: step-4 full flow: no NotFound error during pod replacement race
test_specifications.story: CI engineer gets reliable memcached-pod-kill chaos test results
test_specifications.expected: [e2e] When the replacement memcached pod is created within the same second as the original pod deletion, the test passes without any 'pods not found' errors — because the script watches the Deployment object (which persists), not individual Pod objects.

review_criteria: No chainsaw wait sub-steps with kind=Pod exist in step 4 of the modified chainsaw-test.yaml (REQ-001)

The script follows the operator-pod-crash Deployment readyReplicas polling pattern: Phase 1 polls readyReplicas dropping, Phase 2 polls readyReplicas returning, using ${READY:-0} defensive defaults and explicit error messages (REQ-002, REQ-003)
All 6 Keystone condition assertions in the step-4 assert sub-step are preserved verbatim from the original test (REQ-004)
The NOTE block about theoretical race and initial-sleep mitigation is removed, and the step-4 comment references CC-0076 (REQ-005)
docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md memcached-pod-kill step table and Design note accurately describe the new Deployment-level approach (REQ-006)
No files outside tests/e2e-chaos/memcached-pod-kill/chainsaw-test.yaml and docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md are modified
implementation_notes:
Architecture decision: Replace Pod-level chainsaw wait (which resolves label selectors to specific pod objects at query time) with Deployment-level readyReplicas polling (which watches the persistent Deployment object). This is the same pattern used by operator-pod-crash/chainsaw-test.yaml:54-85 and api-pod-kill-pdb/chainsaw-test.yaml:72-98.

Data flow after fix:
1. Step 3: PodChaos applied -> Chaos Mesh kills one memcached pod
2. Step 4, Phase 1: Script polls Deployment.status.readyReplicas -> detects drop (chaos confirmed)
3. Step 4, Phase 2: ReplicaSet creates replacement -> replacement passes readiness -> readyReplicas returns
4. Step 4, Assert: All 6 Keystone conditions asserted True (no-regression pattern)
5. Step 5: PodChaos deleted
6. Step 6: Final Keystone AllReady assertion

Potential pitfalls:
- Do NOT use set -euo pipefail — ${READY:-0} pattern fails under set -e when kubectl returns empty output (documented in operator-pod-crash comment block)
- Use the Deployment name openstack-memcached (from pod name openstack-memcached-75845cbcd5-ht4h9 and cache.clusterRef.name in 00-keystone-cr.yaml)
- Script timeout (120s) must cover both Phase 1 (30 iterations * 2s = 60s max) and Phase 2 (60 iterations * 2s = 120s max)
- Keep the assert sub-step and catch block completely unchanged — only the wait operations and comments change

Deferred items:
- Investigate if any other chaos tests have similar wait-on-Pod races (operator-pod-crash already uses Deployment polling, so no issue there)
- Upstream contribution to chainsaw/kubectl to support label-selector re-resolution in wait command
- Consider adding a CI retry mechanism for chaos tests as an additional reliability layer
